output:
T3 complete. Added two async storage methods to storage_handler.py after get_topic_editor_message_context:

1. **search_messages_unified** (lines 1384-1534): Archive search with Discord-style filters. Two-pass approach — indexed SQL filters (author, channel, time, reply-status, query, mentions via .or_/.ilike) first, then Python post-filter for jsonb attachment/embed has-checks. Uses _parse_time_bounds for 30-day window clamping. Attaches channel context and author snapshots via existing helpers. Result capped at ~2KB with truncated flag.

2. **get_reply_chain** (lines 1540-1634): Iterative reply-chain walking via reference_id. Cycle detection via seen set. Max depth clamped 1-15. Root-first ordering via compact.reverse(). One SQL query per step.

Also added module-level helpers: _media_check (content_type/extension detection), _parse_single_bound (ISO/relative time parsing), _parse_time_bounds (30-day clamping). Added imports: re, Set, timezone.

Tests: 35/35 non-deferred tests green (10 media+core, 25 live_update_prompts). 2 expected T5-deferred failures unchanged.
deviations: Used frozenset instead of set for _IMAGE_EXT/_VIDEO_EXT/_AUDIO_EXT (immutable — safer at module level)
Added _parse_single_bound helper to separate single-value parsing from _parse_time_bounds window clamping logic for cleaner composition
No deviations from watch_items — all constraints verified
Advisory quality: src/common/storage_handler.py grew by 325 lines (threshold 200).
Advisory quality: code changes lacked test updates: src/common/storage_handler.py.

task_updates.executor_notes: Added module-level helpers (_media_check, _parse_single_bound, _parse_time_bounds) with frozenset extensions for image/video/audio detection. Added async search_messages_unified with two-pass filtering: indexed SQL filters (author, channel, time, reply-status, query, mentions via .or_/.ilike) first, Python post-filter for jsonb attachment/embed has checks. Uses _attach_channel_context_and_filter_nsfw and get_author_context_snapshots helpers. Result capped at ~2KB with truncated flag. Added async get_reply_chain with iterative reference_id walking, cycle detection (seen set), max_depth clamp 1-15, and root-first ordering via compact.reverse(). Both methods include proper error handling. Syntax check passes. All 35 non-deferred tests green (10 media+core, 25 live_update_prompts). Only 2 expected T5-deferred failures remain in test_topic_editor_runtime.
watch_items_verified: mentions_author_id uses .ilike()/.or_() on content with <@ID> and <@!ID> patterns — confirmed no non-existent mentions column
Archive has filtering uses two-pass approach: indexed SQL filters first, Python post-filter for jsonb — confirmed
30-day archive range cap: _parse_time_bounds clamps silently by narrowing the interval — confirmed
One SQL query per reply-chain depth step — confirmed (.maybe_single().execute per iteration)
Result payloads compact: content_preview capped at 200 chars, JSON capped at ~2KB — confirmed
Uses self._as_json_array for normalizing attachments/embeds jsonb — confirmed
Uses existing _attach_channel_context_and_filter_nsfw and get_author_context_snapshots helpers — confirmed
reference_id mapped to reply_to_message_id in tool output — confirmed

sense_check_acknowledgments.note: mentions_author_id filtering uses supabase .or_() with .ilike() on the content column checking for '<@ID>' and '<@!ID>' token patterns. The integer ID is cast to int before formatting into fixed token patterns — no reference to any non-existent mentions column.
sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: mentions_author_id filtering uses supabase .or_() with .ilike() on the content column checking for '<@ID>' and '<@!ID>' token patterns. The integer ID is cast to int before formatting into fixed token patterns — no reference to any non-existent mentions column.

sense_check_acknowledgments.note: get_reply_chain walks reference_id iteratively with: (1) for-loop bounded by safe_depth (max 15), (2) breaks on null reference_id, (3) cycle detection via seen set of message_ids initialized with starting message_id, (4) compact.reverse() for root-first ordering. One SQL query per depth step via .maybe_single().execute().
